title: Share of people who identify as religious or spiritual
caption:
Share of people who identify as religious or spiritual

In 2023, the distribution of religious and spiritual identification varies significantly across the 24 countries shown, with responses categorized into five groups: Religious, Both religious and spiritual, Spiritual, Neither religious nor spiritual, and Did not answer. India displays the largest share identifying strictly as Religious, with the dark blue segment extending past the 60% mark, followed by a substantial portion identifying as Both religious and spiritual. Nigeria and Turkey also show high shares in the Religious category, both exceeding 50%. In contrast, Japan and Australia have the smallest shares identifying as Religious, with dark blue segments remaining below 10%. The category for Neither religious nor spiritual, shown in pink, dominates the responses in Sweden, Spain, and the United Kingdom, where these segments occupy more than half of the total bar length. The United States and Mexico show a different pattern, with the Spiritual category (dark green) representing a significant portion, roughly 30% to 40% of the population in each. Several countries, including Brazil, Kenya, and Tanzania, exhibit a more even distribution across the Religious, Both, and Spiritual categories, with no single group clearly dominating the others. The share of respondents who Did not answer is generally small across all listed nations, typically appearing as a thin grey sliver at the far right of each bar.
